blocks.content: After practice Jonathan walked me home. We didn't take the long way. Just the direct route, same as before. But it didn't feel like before.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: She asked if I wanted to read her paper.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Yeah. She wrote about trust. Like actual trust — between a QB and a receiver. How it builds and what it looks like from outside.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: No. I don't think it is.
He was quieter than usual. Not sad — something more like a person standing at the edge of something and not sure yet whether to step forward.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Yeah. If she wanted you to read it that's why she showed up twice.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: ....I hate when you're right about something.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Get in the habit of it.

blocks.prompt: Jonathan is standing at the edge of something. You can see it.

options.label: "She showed up twice. You just have to show up once."
